mnemonics: The Full STACK: Server, Templating, APIs, Client, Knowledge (of both ends)
keyPoints: Full Stack Development involves working on both the front-end (client-side) and back-end (server-side) of an application
A common tech stack might include HTML/CSS/JavaScript for the front-end and a language like Python, Node.js, or Ruby for the back-end
Understanding how the front-end and back-end communicate (typically via APIs) is fundamental to full stack development
commonMistakes: Neglecting security practices on either end of the application (e.g., input validation, authentication)
Poor communication between front-end and back-end teams leading to integration issues
Over-optimizing one part of the stack while neglecting performance bottlenecks in the other
